title: hieracium murorum subsp. tanyglochin
constituents: Hieracium murorum subsp. tanyglochin has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, alkaloids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. These medicinal constituents, including sesquiterpenes, phenolic glycosides, and coumarins, can help improve people's daily lives by reducing the severity of allergic reactions, promoting wound healing, and protecting against cell damage caused by free radicals. The presence of flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, can also help alleviate symptoms of respiratory disorders, while the alkaloids and phenolic acids in Hieracium murorum subsp. tanyglochin may exhibit antiviral and anticancer activities. By harnessing the potential of these medicinal constituents, individuals can lead healthier and more resilient lives, benefiting from improved immune function, reduced oxidative stress, and enhanced overall well-being.
side_effects: Hieracium murorum subsp. tanyglochin can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal irritation, nausea, and vomiting due to its toxic compounds, particularly sesquiterpene lactones. Ingestion of large quantities may also lead to diarrhea, stomach cramps, and abdominal pain. Additionally, improper use can result in allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, itching, and respiratory issues, including coughing and shortness of breath. Prolonged exposure to its toxic compounds may also cause kidney and liver damage in severe cases.
